target.title: fix(openrouter): reconcile streamed cost with /generation total_cost
target.body:
## Summary

- Wrap the OpenRouter stream at its outermost layer with a fail-open reconciliation step that, after the chat-completion stream ends, fetches `GET /api/v1/generation?id=<responseId>` and overwrites `message.usage.cost.total` with the authoritative billed amount when it is larger than the streamed estimate.
- Add a fail-open `/generation` client + reconciliation helper in `extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts`; compose it as the outermost wrapper in `wrapOpenRouterProviderStream` so it sees the final assistant message produced by every downstream payload-patch wrapper.
- Out of scope: tier-pricing tables (OpenRouter owns those), schema migrations (cost field shape is unchanged), per-call enable/disable config, async/background reconciliation that would touch session storage after the turn completes.

## Linked context

Closes #68066

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request; this follows ClawSweeper's triage that flagged the issue as a `queueable-fix` + `source-repro` + `fix-shape-clear` candidate with the reporter's own Option 1 patch shape (post-hoc `/generation` lookup + overwrite).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Tier-priced OpenRouter models (long-context Qwen tiers in the report) bill substantially more than the streamed `usage.cost.total` exposed in the final chunk. The OpenClaw gateway previously wrote that base-tier value straight into the assistant transcript, so JSONL session cost totals were a 4× undercount on the reported long-context calls and roughly 50% under at the aggregate-traffic level.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux with Node `v22.22.3`, pnpm `11.2.2` via corepack.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local OpenClaw source smoke with `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" node --import tsx --input-type=module -e '...'` against the real `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` helper, exercising each documented failure mode against the four cases ClawSweeper called out (tier-priced long context, short-context parity, HTTP failure, missing responseId).
- Evidence after fix: Copied console output from that local OpenClaw source smoke:

  ```
  === AFTER-FIX BEHAVIOR ===
  Long-context tier-priced: {\"status\":\"updated\",\"previousCost\":0.13342,\"updatedCost\":0.53366} final cost: 0.53366
  Short-context parity   : {\"status\":\"skipped\",\"reason\":\"authoritative total_cost 0.00628 not greater than streamed cost 0.00628\"} final cost: 0.00628
  [openrouter-stream] openrouter cost reconciliation skipped: HTTP 503 from /generation?id=gen-410k-token-call
  HTTP failure fallback  : {\"status\":\"skipped\",\"reason\":\"generation lookup returned no usable total_cost\"} final cost: 0.13342
  No responseId          : {\"status\":\"skipped\",\"reason\":\"missing responseId\"} final cost: 0.13342
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The long-context tier-priced 410k-token call (the exact scenario in the report) now reconciles from the streamed `0.13342` to the authoritative `0.53366` — recovering the 4× undercount end-to-end through the real `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` boundary. Short-context parity, HTTP failure, and missing-responseId paths all degrade to the streamed value with a warn-level log instead of mutating the cost.
- What was not tested: A live call against the real `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/generation` endpoint. The fix is at the OpenRouter stream wrapper boundary; the smoke above uses the same boundary helpers with a stub `fetch` to demonstrate the exact assistant-message mutation downstream session storage sees.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The after-fix proof uses the real `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` from the source checkout, but does not exercise the network call to OpenRouter because this environment is not configured with OpenRouter credentials. The reporter's evidence in the issue body (six `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` calls, with a 4× ratio at 410k tokens and parity below the tier threshold) demonstrates the upstream side of the same boundary.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Captured below via `git stash`-ing this patch on the same branch:

  ```
  === BEFORE-FIX BEHAVIOR ===
  extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts does not exist on main:
    exists? false
  wrapOpenRouterProviderStream exports: wrapOpenRouterProviderStream
  main wraps only payload patchers (anthropic prefill, deepseek thinking, provider routing)
  no /generation lookup or cost reconciliation exists on main
  ```

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.test.ts`
- `PATH=\"\$HOME/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.3/bin:\$PATH\" corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.test.ts extensions/openrouter/stream.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Outputs:

- `cost-reconciliation.test.ts`: `Test Files 1 passed (1)` / `Tests 10 passed (10)` — covers the tier-priced overwrite, both flat and `data`-wrapped `/generation` payloads, short-context parity, missing responseId, missing API key, HTTP error, network rejection, malformed payload, and the stream-wrapper integration (mutating the done-event message in place so iteration and `result()` agree).
- `oxfmt --check`: `All matched files use the correct format.`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added `extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.test.ts` with `reconcileOpenRouterUsageCost` cases for each documented failure mode plus a `createOpenRouterCostReconciliationWrapper` integration case that proves the wrapper mutates the assistant message in place so both event iteration and `result()` observe the reconciled cost.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The before-fix capture above shows `extensions/openrouter/cost-reconciliation.ts` does not exist on main and `wrapOpenRouterProviderStream` composes only payload-patching wrappers, so the regression tests' overwrite assertions would not be possible without this patch.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes (transcript cost only). Sessions that include OpenRouter tier-priced calls will now record the authoritative billed `total_cost` instead of the streamed base-tier estimate. Models without tier pricing observe no change because the authoritative value equals the streamed one and the reconciliation is skipped.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. The fix reuses the existing OpenRouter API key resolved by the gateway and the existing `OPENROUTER_BASE_URL` from `extensions/openrouter/provider-catalog.ts`. No new env vars, config keys, or schema fields.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Network: yes, but narrowly. The reconciliation adds at most one `GET https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/generation?id=<responseId>` call per OpenRouter chat-completion stream, authenticated with the same API key the request used. There is an 8s timeout and the call fails open so a flaky lookup never breaks a turn. Auth/secrets and tool execution: no change.

What is the highest-risk area?

Latency: the reconciliation adds 50-500ms (per the reporter's own measurement) to the moment the agent observes the `done` event for OpenRouter calls. Because the wrapper mutates the message in place before forwarding, the agent loop sees the reconciled cost but waits for the lookup.

How is that risk mitigated?

The 8s controller-driven timeout caps worst-case latency, the lookup is skipped when the streamed cost is already in parity with the authoritative value (so non-tier-priced models avoid the overhead in practice once the cache pattern lands), and every failure mode (timeout, HTTP error, malformed payload, missing API key, missing responseId, parity result) degrades to a warn log instead of mutating the cost or rejecting the turn. The reconciliation only ever revises cost.total upward; it never lowers it, which matches OpenRouter's tier-priced billing direction.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await maintainer / ClawSweeper review on (a) whether `usage.cost.total` overwrite is the preferred shape over the alternative \"keep both\" approach the reporter listed as Option 2, and (b) whether the reconciliation should be opt-in per-model.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

A live `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` long-context smoke against the real OpenRouter `/generation` endpoint is still not provided from this environment.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Initial PR; no PR comments yet.

AI-assisted: yes. I reviewed the touched code paths, designed the stream-wrapper boundary, and ran the focused validation commands above.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

target.title: Feat/discord slash command deploy 75888
target.body:
## Summary
Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:
If this PR fixes a plugin beta-release blocker, title it `fix(<plugin-id>): beta blocker - <summary>` and link the matching `Beta blocker: <plugin-name> - <summary>` issue labeled `beta-blocker`. Contributors cannot label PRs, so the title is the PR-side signal for maintainers and automation.
- **Problem:** Discord native slash-command deploy lacked a configurable lifecycle policy (`always` / `changed-only` / `disabled`), durable fingerprints across gateway restarts for `changed-only`, safe in-process coordination when multiple Discord accounts trigger background slash deploy from one gateway. Separately: Vitest 4 removed **`TestRunner`** from **`vitest`**, breaking the repo custom non-isolated runner; Discord provider harness mocks needed the new **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** export.
- **Why it matters:** Fewer redundant Discord application-command REST calls when unchanged (rate-limit / churn), explicit opt-out (`disabled`), restart-stable `changed-only` semantics, and predictable multi-account deploy ordering; tests must boot on current Vitest.
- **What changed (product/code):** `channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy` accepts string shorthand **or** `{ mode }` (default **`changed-only`** when omitted); merge persist successful deploy fingerprints; preload deployer snapshot for `changed-only`; queue per-process background deploy; docs (`docs/channels/discord.md`), changelog, config UI hints, core types + zod schema + plugin-sdk runtime helper alignment, regenerated `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256`, new/updated Discord extension tests around deploy state and wiring.
- **What changed (test infra):** `test/non-isolated-runner.ts` extends **`VitestTestRunner`** from **`vitest/runners`**; `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts` mocks **`resolveDiscordSlashCommandDeployConfig`** for `monitor/commands.js` partial mock compatibility.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Non-Discord channels/providers; unrelated gateway loops beyond shared config/schema surfaces required for the new key.
_AI-assisted._ Local verification: targeted Discord extension Vitest shard (see **Repro + Verification → Actual**).
## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra
## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra
## Linked Issue/PR
- Closes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/75888
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression (issue-tracked behavior gaps for Discord slash deploy policy + persistence + cleanup)
## Root Cause (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.
- **Root cause:** N/A for a single accidental line bug—this PR implements the missing Discord deploy policy + persistence model described in #75888, plus modernizes the custom Vitest runner for Vitest 4 exports and updates incomplete module mocks after adding new command helpers.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** No durable fingerprint + explicit deploy policy previously; tests did not cover the new export on a partially mocked `monitor/commands.js` module.
- **Contributing context (if known):** #75888 requested configurable deploy strategy and cross-restart fingerprinting with safe defaults and cleanup semantics.
## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** `extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts`, `extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts`, plus harness `extensions/discord/src/test-support/provider.test-support.ts`.
- **Scenario the test should lock in:** `slashCommandDeploy` parse/default; fingerprint merge semantics; provider monitor wiring with mocked command helpers including deploy resolver; schema acceptance for new config shape.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** Exercises contracts without live Discord credentials.
- **Existing test that already covers this (if any):** N/A beyond files above.
- **If no new test is added, why not:** N/A
## User-visible / Behavior Changes
Optional **`channels.discord.slashCommandDeploy`**: **`"always" | "changed-only" | "disabled"`** **or** **`{ mode: ... }`**. Default when omitted: **`changed-only`**. **`changed-only`** persists successful deploy fingerprints across restarts to skip redundant REST deploy when command definitions are unchanged. Multi-account Discord background slash deploy is serialized per gateway process. Docs/changelog describe behavior; config baseline hash regenerated for schema/docs drift checks.
Docs hub (sync lag may apply): https://docs.openclaw.ai/channels/discord
## Diagram (if applicable)
For UI changes or non-trivial logic flows, include a small ASCII diagram reviewers can scan quickly. Otherwise write `N/A`.
```text
N/A
```
## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **Yes**
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Writes/reads local gateway persistent state for non-secret Discord slash deploy fingerprint metadata (keyed per Discord application/account pairing as implemented).
## Repro + Verification
### Environment
- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord extension Vitest project (`extension-discord`) — no live Discord token required for this command
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A
### Steps
1. From the OpenClaw repository root, ensure dependencies are installed (`pnpm install`).
2. Run:
```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose 2>&1 | tee proof-discord-verbose.txt
```
### Expected
The command exits **0**. Vitest reports **7** passing test files and **127** passing tests for the `extension-discord` shard (see **Actual**).
### Actual
```
> openclaw@2026.6.1 test
> node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts

  RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/liminglin/Funny/openclaw

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:38:27
   Duration  5.65s (transform 3.58s, setup 286ms, import 4.32s, tests 921ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 8.54s
```
Process exit code: **0**
## Evidence
Attach at least one:
- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (Vitest 4 runner bootstrap fixed; missing mock export fixed; green shard output above)
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)
## Human Verification (required)
What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:
- **Verified scenarios:** Ran the exact **`pnpm test …`** command in **Steps**; confirmed **Actual** summary matches terminal (**7** files, **127** tests, exit **0**); reviewed `slashCommandDeploy` docs + schema defaults for consistency with parser.
- **Edge cases checked:** `disabled`, `always`, `changed-only`; multi-account queue assumption at coordinator level.
- **What you did **not** verify:** Live production Discord bot/guild roundtrip beyond extension/unit coverage (additional confidence from PR CI).
## Review Conversations
- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.
## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **Yes**
- Migration needed? **No**
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A (operators who need prior implicit “always redeploy every restart” behavior can set `slashCommandDeploy: "always"` explicitly).
## Risks and Mitigations
- **Risk:** Fleet-wide cold start may still issue full deploys until first successful fingerprint write exists per application/account pairing.
  - **Mitigation:** `changed-only` amortizes after first success; `disabled` / `always` explicit operator posture.

## Real behavior proof

  - Behavior addressed:
    Discord `slashCommandDeploy=changed-only` persists deploy fingerprints in Discord plugin state (SQLite) and skips redundant Discord application-command REST on gateway restart when the serialized command set is unchanged.

  - Real environment tested:
    macOS (darwin arm64), Node v24.14.0; **live Discord gateway** on branch `feat/discord-slash-command-deploy-75888` @ **`7bf4b50`** (`OpenClaw 2026.6.1` local build), guild `1471499665584296039`, application `1471498116237885474`, `--profile discord-proof`, token via `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` env (not pasted in PR).

  - Exact steps or command run after this patch:
    1. `pnpm build`
    2. `source ~/.env/third-party-apis.zsh && export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-discord-proof`
    3. **Prep (cold fingerprint):** `sqlite3 ~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite "DELETE FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE plugin_id='discord' AND namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"`
    4. **RUN1:** `node openclaw.mjs --log-level debug --profile discord-proof gateway run --verbose --port 19099 --force` — wait for `deploy-commands:done`
    5. Stop gateway; verify plugin-state fingerprint row exists (see Evidence)
    6. **RUN2:** rerun the same gateway command unchanged — wait for `deploy-commands:done`

  - Evidence after fix:
    Terminal transcript (`pnpm test … --reporter=verbose`) ends with `Test Files 7 passed`, `Tests 127 passed`, exit **0**.

    **Live gateway @ `16ddd7f2` (local transcript, token redacted):**
    - **Startup migration (first boot on this head with legacy JSON present):** gateway auto-migrated `discord/slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` → plugin state and archived legacy source to `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json.migrated`.
    - **RUN1 (empty plugin-state fingerprint, commands already registered on Discord):** `deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47`; `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get` to `/applications/1471498116237885474/commands`; `deploy-commands:done` ~4.8s; fingerprint persisted in plugin state:
      `sqlite3 … "SELECT entry_key, value_json FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"` → `1471498116237885474:default` with `global:reconcile` SHA256 `0adc06bf…9297aa9`.
    - **RUN2 (unchanged config/commands):** `deploy-commands:schedule` + `deploy-commands:done` ~1.6s; **zero** `native-slash-command-deploy-rest:*` lines (REST deploy skipped).

    Key log lines (RUN1):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:start … path=/applications/1471498116237885474/commands
native-slash-command-deploy-rest:get:done … requestMs=715
deploy-commands:done … ~4792ms
```

    Key log lines (RUN2):
```
deploy-commands:schedule … slashCommandDeploy=changed-only … commandCount=47
deploy-commands:done … ~1659ms
(no native-slash-command-deploy-rest:* lines)
```

  - Observed result after fix:
    - `changed-only` reads/writes fingerprints in **Discord plugin state** (`openKeyedStore`, namespace `slash-command-deploy-hashes`, key `applicationId:accountId`) under `~/.openclaw-discord-proof/state/openclaw.sqlite`.
    - After fingerprint exists, immediate gateway restart skips Discord deploy REST while still logging `deploy-commands:scheduled` / `deploy-commands:done`.
    - Legacy JSON is migrated via startup/doctor path only; runtime steady-state does not read the JSON sidecar.

  - What was not tested:
    - `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
    - Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit
    - Multi-account serial deploy queue under live dual-bot startup

  - Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
    Prior behavior: every gateway restart unconditionally reconciled slash commands via Discord REST even when the command list was unchanged.



---

## Migration proof (commits b3282460 + 16ddd7f2)

**What was added after original live proof:**

Two follow-up commits resolve the ClawSweeper P1 finding: shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json` (`{ hashes }` schema, no appId/accountId) was not migrated to plugin state on upgrade.

**Fix:**
- `slash-command-deploy-state.ts`: added `DISCORD_COMMAND_DEPLOY_CACHE_LEGACY_JSON_RELATIVE_PATH` constant
- `model-picker-preferences-migrations.ts`: new migration plan reads `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`, calls `mergeDiscordAccountConfig` per account to resolve both root-inherited and per-account `applicationId`, then writes one plugin-state entry per `applicationId:accountId` pair. `shouldReplaceExistingEntry: () => false` prevents overwriting a fresher `slash-command-deploy-hashes.json` migration.

**Test run at head `7bf4b50` (macOS, Node v22.19.0, Vitest v4.1.7):**

```
pnpm test \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences-migrations.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/slash-command-deploy-state.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/internal/client.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/commands.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/config-schema.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.startup.test.ts \
  extensions/discord/src/monitor/provider.test.ts \
  -- --reporter=verbose

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  127 passed (127)
   Start at  15:23:00
   Duration  3.17s
```

**Migration scenarios covered by new tests:**
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with root `applicationId` → single `appId:default` key
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with no `applicationId` → empty entries (graceful cold start)
- `command-deploy-cache.json` with two accounts each having distinct per-account `applicationId` → two keys `111100001111:bot1` and `222200002222:bot2`

**Doctor upgrade proof (current-head `7bf4b50`, Node v22.19.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.1):**

Simulated v2026.5.28 upgrade from scratch state dir with shipped `discord/command-deploy-cache.json`:

```
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=~/.openclaw-proof-upgrade openclaw doctor --fix --yes --non-interactive
```

Doctor output:
```
[state-migrations] Auto-migrated legacy state:
- Migrated 1 Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) entry → plugin state
- Archived Discord command deploy cache (v2026.5.28 and earlier) legacy source →
  command-deploy-cache.json.migrated
```

SQLite verification after migration:
```
sqlite3 ... "SELECT plugin_id, namespace, entry_key, value_json
  FROM plugin_state_entries WHERE namespace='slash-command-deploy-hashes';"
→ discord|slash-command-deploy-hashes|111122223333:default|
  {"version":1,"hashes":{"global:reconcile":"aaa...a",
  "guild:1471499665584296039":"bbb...b"}}
```

Legacy file status:
```
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json       # → No such file (migrated)
$ ls discord/command-deploy-cache.json.migrated  # → archived copy exists
```

**Combined proof:**
- Doctor --fix migrates v2026.5.28 shipped `command-deploy-cache.json` → per-account plugin state entries (`applicationId:accountId` key format) with correct hash passthrough and proper archival of the legacy source.
- Existing live gateway proof (above) confirms `changed-only` reads fingerprints from this same plugin state namespace and skips Discord deploy REST when unchanged on subsequent restart.

**What was not tested:**
- Multi-account live gateway restart post-upgrade (simulated config with single default account only)
- `slashCommandDeploy: "disabled"` on live gateway restart
- Live Discord 429 / `retry_after` under rate limit

target.title: feat: Provider/Cohere onboarding + auth-choice support
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Cohere was missing as a first-class onboarding/auth choice, so users had to hand-roll custom provider config.
- Why it matters: this made Cohere setup inconsistent with other supported providers and harder to script in non-interactive onboarding.
- What changed: added built-in `cohere-api-key` onboarding flow, provider defaults (`cohere/command-a-03-2025` + base URL), auth/env resolution, CLI flags/docs, and tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no changes to memory embeddings provider support, no new transport/protocol logic, and no channel behavior changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #23845

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New auth choice: `cohere-api-key`.
- New onboarding flag: `--cohere-api-key`.
- Non-interactive onboarding can now infer Cohere from `--cohere-api-key`.
- Default Cohere model/config is applied as:
  - provider id: `cohere`
  - base URL: `https://api.cohere.ai/compatibility/v1`
  - default model: `cohere/command-a-03-2025`
- `models status` now probes `COHERE_API_KEY` in env auth visibility.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes`)
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Adds Cohere API-key profile storage and env-key resolution (`COHERE_API_KEY`) using existing auth-profile storage paths and key normalization logic.
  - Adds Cohere provider base URL defaults; request execution still goes through existing OpenAI-compatible provider plumbing.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+, pnpm
- Model/provider: Cohere (`cohere/command-a-03-2025`)
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): generated via onboarding tests

### Steps

1. Run `openclaw onboard --non-interactive --auth-choice cohere-api-key --cohere-api-key "$COHERE_API_KEY"`.
2. Inspect generated config/auth profiles.
3. Run auth/onboarding test suite.

### Expected

- Cohere auth profile + provider defaults are configured and default model is `cohere/command-a-03-2025`.

### Actual

- Matches expected behavior; tests pass.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `pnpm test -- src/commands/onboard-auth.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice-options.test.ts src/commands/onboard-non-interactive.provider-auth.test.ts src/cli/program/register.onboard.test.ts src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts`
  - `pnpm format`
  - `pnpm check`
- Edge cases checked:
  - token-provider mapping path (`apiKey + tokenProvider=cohere`)
  - non-interactive auth-choice inference from `--cohere-api-key`
  - provider alias/default model merge behavior
- What you did **not** verify:
  - live Cohere API call against production account in this branch

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`Yes`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
  - use a different `--auth-choice` and remove `models.providers.cohere` + `auth.profiles.cohere:default` entries.
- Files/config to restore:
  - `openclaw.json` model/auth sections and auth profile store entries for `cohere:default`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - missing default model assignment after Cohere onboarding
  - auth-choice mismatch for `--cohere-api-key`

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Cohere compatibility endpoint or default model metadata drifts over time.
  - Mitigation: docs + constants are centralized; follow-up model metadata updates are isolated and test-covered.
- Risk: auth-choice catalog mismatch across CLI/docs/non-interactive inference.
  - Mitigation: shared flag catalog + coverage in onboarding/auth-choice tests.

## Notes

- AI-assisted: yes.

target.title: fix: recover terminal session status on visible inbound turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #86827 — group chat sessions stuck in `failed` status silently drop all subsequent messages.

When a group chat session enters a terminal status (`failed`, `timeout`, or `killed`), the reply dispatch path returns a successful no-op (`queuedFinal: false`, zero dispatch counts) without starting an AI turn or delivering any user-visible error. All subsequent messages are silently discarded until a manual `/new` reset.

## Root Cause

Three independent mechanisms conspire to create the silent drop:

1. **Session state persistence**: `initSessionState()` reuses the existing session entry with its terminal `status` field intact, passing it downstream unchanged.
2. **Reply operation locking**: A stale `ReplyOperation` from the failed run remains registered in `replyRunRegistry`, causing `admitReplyTurn()` to return `skipped` with reason `active-run`.
3. **No fallback or diagnostic**: The `skipped` admission produces a valid dispatch result with zero counts, and no warning is logged — the message simply vanishes.

## Changes

### 1. Auto-recover terminal sessions (`session.ts`)

When a **visible** (non-cron/heartbeat) inbound message hits a session with recoverable terminal status, automatically clear the stale lifecycle fields (`status`, `startedAt`, `endedAt`, `runtimeMs`, `abortedLastRun`) while **preserving the `sessionId` and transcript continuity** — no session rotation.

### 2. Clear stale reply operations with race guard (`dispatch-from-config.ts`)

When `admitReplyTurn()` returns `skipped` with reason `active-run` but the persisted session is in terminal status, force-clear the stale reply operation via `forceClearReplyRunBySessionId()` and retry admission **once**. This breaks the deadlock without risking infinite retry loops.

**Concurrent recovery guard**: After a recovery turn clears the proven stale leftover and admits its own fresh operation, it is marked with a `terminalRecovery` flag. If a second visible message arrives against the same terminal snapshot before the first recovery clears the store state, the force-clear branch now skips operations marked as in-flight terminal recoveries. This prevents a sibling recovery turn from killing a fresh recovery that is already in flight, letting concurrent turns fall through to normal busy/wait handling instead.

### 3. Agent API path (`agent.ts`)

Mirror the same terminal recovery logic in the agent API dispatch path so agent API requests also clear terminal state for visible requests.

### 4. Diagnostics (`kernel.ts`, `types.ts`)

Add a structured warning log when a visible channel turn dispatch completes with `queuedFinal: false` and zero tool/block/final counts — a sentinel for future silent message drops. Excludes system turns (heartbeat/cron/exec) and observe-only admissions.

## Real behavior proof

Captured by driving the **real** runtime modules (`dispatchReplyFromConfig`, `initSessionState`, `reply-run-registry`) with verbose logging on, **no vitest and no module mocks** — only the production parameters the Gateway supplies plus a real on-disk JSON session store. A before/after contrast was produced by running the identical driver in a pre-fix worktree.

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: A group-chat session left in a terminal lifecycle status (`failed`/`timeout`/`killed`) silently drops every later message — the stale lifecycle entry plus a leftover "active" reply operation block turn admission, so a subsequent visible inbound message gets no AI turn and no reply (#86827).
- **Real environment tested**: Compiled branch source at HEAD `ac52eb0b24`; macOS 15.3.1 (Darwin 24.3.0, arm64), Node `v24.10.0`. Product built with `pnpm build:docker` (exit 0); the recovery log string is present in compiled `dist/`. Evidence captured via `tsx` driving the real in-process dispatch/session/registry path. Before/after contrast run at pre-fix commit `8de37e1ce4` (parent of the first PR commit).
- **Failing proof before fix** (`8de37e1ce4`, identical driver + input): real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible group turn on a `status="failed"` session with a stale running reply op —
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  [dispatch] dispatch TIMED-OUT (no reply within 8s): visible turn stuck behind the stale terminal reply op -> SILENT DROP
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = null
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = true
  ```
  And real `initSessionState()` against an on-disk store: `RESULT: STILL-TERMINAL — session entry remains status=failed after a visible inbound turn`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build:docker
  export OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d) OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 \
         OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1 OPENCLAW_SKIP_CRON=1
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts   # drives real dispatchReplyFromConfig()
  node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_proof_driver.mts      # drives real initSessionState() + registry
  ```
  The driver seeds a real session store with a terminal `failed` group entry, registers a real stale running reply operation, then calls the real `dispatchReplyFromConfig()` for a visible inbound group message.
- **Evidence after fix (`ac52eb0b24`)**: copied live console output from running the real in-process driver `node_modules/.bin/tsx ./_dispatch_driver.mts` (drives the production `dispatchReplyFromConfig()`); redacted runtime log:
  ```
  [dispatch] registered stale running reply op; isActive=true
  [dispatch] calling REAL dispatchReplyFromConfig() for visible group turn on terminal session ...
  dispatch-from-config: cleared stale active reply operation for terminal session agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted>
  [dispatch] dispatch returned: {"queuedFinal":true,"counts":{"tool":0,"block":0,"final":1}}
  [dispatch] stale op result after dispatch = {"kind":"failed","code":"run_failed"}
  [dispatch] registry isActive after dispatch = false
  ```
  Real session lifecycle: `initSessionState -> isNewSession=false sessionId=<same> abortedLastRun=false`, and `AFTER turn -> status=undefined ... RESULT: RECOVERED — terminal lifecycle cleared, same sessionId reused (transcript preserved)`.
- **Observed result after fix**: A visible inbound message to a terminal session (1) emits the new recovery log, (2) force-fails the stale leftover op and frees the registry (`isActive=false`), (3) produces a real queued reply (`queuedFinal:true, counts.final:1`) instead of the pre-fix zero-count silent drop / indefinite block, and (4) clears the persisted terminal lifecycle fields while reusing the same `sessionId` (transcript preserved). The pre-fix checkout, given identical input, leaves the session `failed` and the turn stuck/dropped.
- **What was not tested**: The full Docker image build failed at the `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` layer due to transient npm-registry CDN errors fetching optional native packages (network, not OOM and not code), so no end-to-end live channel socket round-trip was performed; the proof drives the real in-process dispatch/session/registry runtime that a channel turn funnels into. The `agent.ts` Gateway RPC path and the `kernel.ts` zero-count warning were source-verified but not separately runtime-exercised here. Concurrency races (sibling visible-turn recovery, heartbeat non-clobber) are covered by the added unit tests. Architecture: arm64 (macOS) only; CI truth is Linux x64 Node 24.

## Tests

- **session.test.ts**: Verify failed group sessions recover without rotating the transcript
- **dispatch-from-config.test.ts**: Verify stale active reply operations are cleared for terminal sessions and admission is retried; verify two concurrent visible turns against the same terminal snapshot do not kill each other's recovery operations
- **agent.test.ts**: Verify terminal failed agent API sessions recover without session rotation; updated existing failed-session tests to verify terminal state clearing
- **kernel.test.ts**: Verify zero-count visible dispatch warning is emitted
- **agent-runner-execution.test.ts / agent-runner-memory.test.ts**: Updated mock `ReplyOperation` stubs with new `terminalRecovery` field and `markTerminalRecovery` method

All focused tests pass across 2 Vitest shards (512 cases).

target.title: feat(memory-wiki): page groups configurability — custom index dirs + recursive scanning
target.body:
## Summary

This PR introduces page group configurability to the memory-wiki plugin, removing hard-coded directory paths and enabling customizable vault directories.

## Real Behavior Proof

### 1. Plugin loads with custom Chinese directory names
Custom .wiki-page-groups.json defines: source->资料, entity->实体, concept->概念, synthesis->综合/视图/模板 (multiple dirs per kind), report->报告

### 2. All vault directories created dynamically from pageGroups

### 3. Compiled index correctly groups pages by custom headings

### 4. Multi-level subdirectories are recursively discovered

### 5. ClawSweeper fix commit 24389561
a) fs-safe writes restored -- writeWikiPage uses fsRoot
b) mergePageGroups precedence -- user tops vault tops defaults
c) pageGroups threaded through all query paths
d) getDefaultDirForKind import fixed

### 6. Round-2 fix commit 45ce5b74
P1-1: mergePageGroups preserves multi-dir per kind
P1-2: vault.ts writeFileIfMissing uses fsRoot(rootDir).create()
P2: tsconfig boundary, recursive query, status pageGroups

### 7. Round-3 fix commit b53cef6d
P2-1: config.test.ts expects report in DEFAULT_PAGE_GROUPS
P2-2: loadExtraPageGroupsFromVault uses Zod safeParse
P2-3: status.ts recursive walkDir + all source dirs

## Real Behavior Proof (updated with live vault data from production OpenClaw setup)

### 1. Custom Chinese directory names via `.wiki-page-groups.json`

The external config file defines source->资料, entity->实体, concept->概念, synthesis->综合/视图/模板 (multiple dirs per kind), report->报告:

```json
{
  "pageGroups": [
    { "kind": "source", "dir": "资料", "heading": "资料" },
    { "kind": "entity", "dir": "实体", "heading": "实体" },
    { "kind": "concept", "dir": "概念", "heading": "概念" },
    { "kind": "synthesis", "dir": "综合", "heading": "综合" },
    { "kind": "synthesis", "dir": "视图", "heading": "视图" },
    { "kind": "synthesis", "dir": "模板", "heading": "模板" },
    { "kind": "report", "dir": "报告", "heading": "报告" }
  ]
}
```

### 2. All directories created dynamically from pageGroups

Vault root contains the following directories, all derived from pageGroups:
- 资料/ (kind: source) -- 719 pages
- 实体/ (kind: entity) -- 236 pages
- 概念/ (kind: concept) -- 298 pages
- 综合/ (kind: synthesis) -- 21 pages
- 视图/ (kind: synthesis) -- 23 pages
- 模板/ (kind: synthesis) -- 1 pages
- 报告/ (kind: report) -- 58 pages

### 3. Recursive subdirectory scanning (multi-level)

资料/ directory has recursive subdirectories up to 3+ levels deep, all discovered automatically:
- 资料/AI/
- 资料/技术/AI/
- 资料/技术/基础设施/
- 资料/心理学/
- 资料/个人/学习方法/
- 资料/个人/思维/
- 资料/庄子/
- 资料/系统科学/
- etc. (130 total directories)

Example nested files under 资料/技术/AI/:
- 资料/技术/AI/2026-05-28-Ctx2Skill-深度解析.md
- 资料/技术/AI/2026-05-17-HarnessEngineering-企业实战90%.md
- 资料/技术/AI/agent-harness-engineering-survey.md

### 4. Compiled index correctly groups pages by custom headings

The compiled index (`index.md`) uses Chinese directory names as wikilink paths:
- [[资料/技术/AI/2026-05-13-anthropic-code-with-claude.md|Anthropic "Code w/ Claude"...]]
- [[实体/人物/xxx.md]]
- [[概念/知识库/xxx.md]]

### 5. Dashboard reports generated under 报告/

All plugin-owned report dashboards are created in the configured 报告/ directory:
- claim-health.md, contradictions.md, stale-pages.md
- draft-verification-YYYY-MM-DD.md
- file-audit-YYYY-MM-DD.md
- vault-health-YYYY-MM-DD.md

### 6. Overall vault statistics
- Total markdown files: 1376
- Total directories: 130
- All directories initialized and indexed via pageGroups-driven vault initialization

## Real Behavior Proof (raw terminal output from production vault)

### .wiki-page-groups.json
```
{
  "pageGroups": [
    { "kind": "source", "dir": "资料", "heading": "资料" },
    { "kind": "entity", "dir": "实体", "heading": "实体" },
    { "kind": "concept", "dir": "概念", "heading": "概念" },
    { "kind": "synthesis", "dir": "综合", "heading": "综合" },
    { "kind": "synthesis", "dir": "视图", "heading": "视图" },
    { "kind": "synthesis", "dir": "模板", "heading": "模板" },
    { "kind": "report", "dir": "报告", "heading": "报告" }
  ]
}
```

### Vault directories (created from pageGroups)

PR surface: Source +114, Tests +917. Total +1031 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection and the linked issue give a clear reproduction chain around released prompt locks, provider retries, and session takeover; I did not run the repro in this read-only review. The PR's real-behavior proof supports the path, but it names an older head SHA rather than the live PR head.

target.title: fix: prevent silent message loss from EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError
target.body:
## Summary

When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally (default `maxRetries=2`), the session write lock was released during the retry window. Incoming steering messages could then modify the transcript without updating the fence, causing a fingerprint mismatch on `reacquireAfterPrompt()`. The resulting `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` was caught by `model-fallback.ts` which aborted the run, and the outer reply pipeline produced no user-visible output — the message was silently lost.

## Root Cause Chain

```
API ECONNRESET
  → OpenAI SDK default maxRetries=2, internal retry
  → session write lock released during retry window
  → incoming message arrives via queueHandle.queueMessage
  → activeSession.steer writes directly (bypassing withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites)
  → transcript changes without updating fence
  → original attempt reacquireAfterPrompt() fingerprint mismatch
  → EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError thrown
  → model-fallback.ts aborts the run
  → outer layer hits generic failure branch
  → assistantTexts/payloads never enter buildReplyPayloads()
  → message silently lost (user sees generic error or nothing)
```

## Fix

Five coordinated changes that address the bug at multiple layers:

### 1. Wrap steering in `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites` (`attempt.ts`)
Steering writes that arrive during prompt lock release now go through `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, keeping fence tracking consistent and preventing the fingerprint mismatch that triggers the takeover error.

### 2. Dedicated error handler for takeover (`agent-runner-execution.ts`, `failover-error.ts`)
Added `isEmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError()` export and a dedicated catch block in `runAgentTurnWithFallback`. Instead of falling into the generic failure branch (silent loss), users now receive a clear message asking them to resend. When a takeover wraps a preserved prompt error (billing, rate-limit, context-overflow), the original error is unwrapped and classified normally — only pure takeover errors return resend guidance.

### 3. Scope SDK retry disable to embedded prompt lock path (`attempt.ts`, `attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Extracted `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` helper that wraps the session's `streamFn` with `{ maxRetries: 0, ...options }` — the `0` is a **default** that goes before the caller options spread, so `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` (surfaced via `options.maxRetries` per the chain in `sdk.ts:360`) takes precedence when explicitly configured. Non-embedded callers retain the SDK default retry behavior (maxRetries=2).

### 4. Forward maxRetries through native OpenAI transport (`openai-transport-stream.ts`)
`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` previously accepted only `model` and `signal`, dropping the `maxRetries` injected by `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault`. Added `maxRetries` parameter and forwarded it through all three native transport call sites (Responses, Azure Responses, Completions). When `maxRetries` is the only option present, the function now returns an object instead of `undefined` so the SDK receives the override.

### 5. Phase tracking in `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` (`attempt.session-lock.ts`)
Added a `phase` field (`prompt_reacquire`, `cleanup`, `write`, `fence_check`) for better diagnostics when this error does occur. Backward compatible — the phase parameter is optional with existing call sites unchanged.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Silent message loss on session takeover during the released prompt-lock window. When the OpenAI SDK retried an `ECONNRESET` internally, an incoming steering write changed the transcript without updating the fence, raising `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, which `model-fallback.ts` aborted into a generic failure branch so the user saw no actionable output. After this fix the pure-takeover path returns explicit resend guidance, the SDK retry default is scoped to the embedded prompt-lock window (with configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` taking precedence), and `maxRetries` is forwarded through the native OpenAI transport.
- **Real environment tested**: Clean Docker container `node:24-bookworm` (Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running the patched checkout at the current head commit `2fc3ecee2d`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Built the patched checkout in the container and ran the affected suites plus a real-setup organic takeover test (real on-disk session file + real local `node:http` model server on an `openai-completions` baseUrl + real transport + real fence) and code-path verification, as captured in the v6 proof log below.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied live terminal output from the patched container at head `2fc3ecee2d` (full v6 log reproduced in the section below):

```
$ docker run --rm -v "$PWD:/app" -w /app node:24-bookworm bash /proof.sh
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v6, current head) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 2fc3ecee2d
--- A: Real-setup organic session-takeover proof (resend guidance) ---
 ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 1456ms
AFTER-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.
BEFORE-fix user-facing payload text (exact, parent 86150a3e51):
⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
=== PROOF COMPLETE @ 2fc3ecee2d ===
```
- **Observed result after fix**: The organic session takeover now surfaces a specific, actionable resend message (the after-fix payload line above) instead of the generic non-actionable failure seen at the parent; the configured provider retry survives through `sdk.ts` to the native SDK request options; the native transport forwards `maxRetries`; and the retry-default installer is idempotent with caller > configured > 0 precedence.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user deployment against a real upstream provider with an actual network-level `ECONNRESET` was run. The takeover proof uses a real on-disk session file, a real local HTTP model server, the real transport, and the real fence (organic error), with only the upstream provider socket and the "second user" steering write synthesized; the supplementary transport proof relies on unit/loopback tests in a real Docker container.

### Current-head proof (commit `2fc3ecee2d`, Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

Refreshed at the latest head, which adds the configured-provider-retry precedence. Run in a clean Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64):

```
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v6, current head) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 2fc3ecee2d

--- A: Real-setup organic session-takeover proof (resend guidance) ---
 ✓ auto-reply  agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts (1 test)
   ✓ surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 1456ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)

--- B: Embedded prompt retry default — idempotence + configured-override precedence ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  51 passed (51)

--- C: Configured provider retry survives through sdk.ts to native SDK options ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)

--- D: Transport forwards maxRetries (native OpenAI) + failover classification ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  83 passed (83)
 Test Files  1 passed (1)    Tests  241 passed (241)

--- E: Code-path verification at current head ---
installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault accepts configured default:
1088:  options?: { maxRetries?: number },
1098:  const defaultMaxRetries = options?.maxRetries ?? 0;
Install site passes configured provider retry:
3994:              maxRetries: settingsManager.getProviderRetrySettings().maxRetries,

=== PROOF COMPLETE @ 2fc3ecee2d ===
```

This confirms at the current head: the organic session-takeover path still yields visible resend guidance (A); the retry-default installer is idempotent and applies the configured-override precedence caller > configured > 0 (B); a configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` survives through `sdk.ts` to the native SDK request options (C, the new regression); and the native transport forwards `maxRetries` (D).

### Real session-takeover path (organic error, real transport + real fence)

This proof drives the actual user-visible takeover path end to end and captures the real user-facing reply, rather than relying only on focused unit tests. Committed as `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts`.

**What is real (not hand-built):**
- A real on-disk session transcript file in a temp dir.
- A real local `node:http` server as the model `baseUrl` (`api: openai-completions`) that announces in-flight, then blocks before finishing the SSE body.
- The real `openai-completions` transport doing real HTTP I/O.
- The real embedded prompt-lock controller + fence (`createEmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController`), wired through the real `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` / `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` helpers — the exact helpers `attempt.ts` uses in production.
- The `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` is thrown **organically** by `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")` when a concurrent steering write changes the session-file fingerprint during the released-lock window — it is **not** constructed by hand.
- The real `isNonProviderRuntimeCoordinationError` classification and the real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch branch producing the user-facing payload.

**Mechanism (deterministic Promise handshakes, no sleeps):**
1. A real temp session file is created with one session line.
2. On the first model request the mock server resolves a `firstRequestReceived` deferred (the model call is in-flight), then blocks on a `releaseServer` deferred before writing the body.
3. `installPromptSubmissionLockRelease` calls `releaseForPrompt()` at the start of the model call (opening the takeover window) and drains the real transport stream, so the released-lock window spans the real HTTP block.
4. While blocked, the test performs a concurrent "second user" steering write to the **same** real session file, changing its fingerprint.
5. `releaseServer` resolves; the server finishes the SSE body; the wrapped `finally{}` calls `reacquireAfterPrompt()` → `assertSessionFileFence("prompt_reacquire")`.
6. The fingerprint mismatch trips the fence → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` thrown organically → real classification → real `runAgentTurnWithFallback` catch.

**Before / after (run at this PR's parent `86150a3e51` in a separate worktree; the session-lock + fence code is byte-identical between parent and HEAD, so the organic error reproduces identically — the only difference is the runner's new catch branch):**

```
AFTER fix (HEAD) — vitest verbose
 RUN  v4.1.7
 ✓ |auto-reply| agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > surfaces resend guidance after an organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError 487ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
AFTER-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Your message was interrupted because new input arrived while the model was retrying a connection error. Please resend your message.

BEFORE fix (parent 86150a3e51, separate worktree) — vitest verbose
 RUN  v4.1.7
 ✓ |auto-reply| agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts > BEFORE FIX: organic EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError yields no resend guidance 483ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
BEFORE-fix user-facing payload text (exact):
⚠️ Something went wrong while processing your request. Please try again, or use /new to start a fresh session.
```

**What this proves:** an organic session takeover during a real released-lock model call produces, after this fix, a specific actionable resend message instead of the generic non-actionable failure the user saw before. Honest note: at this PR's immediate parent the before-fix payload is the **generic** failure text shown above (not literal empty output); the originally reported "silent message loss" is the production symptom of that non-actionable failure path. The change at this seam converts the generic failure into specific resend guidance so the user knows their steering message must be resent.

### Supplementary: transport + retry-suppression proof (Docker `node:24-bookworm`)

- **Behavior addressed**: Silent message loss when SDK retries race with session takeover → visible resend guidance for pure takeover, correct error classification for wrapped prompt errors, provider retry settings respected, maxRetries forwarded through native OpenAI transport
- **Real environment tested**: Docker container (`node:24-bookworm`, Node.js `v24.16.0`, Debian GNU/Linux 12 bookworm, aarch64) running patched checkout at commit `80bbe803e2`
- **Failing proof before fix**: On upstream/main, `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` only accepts `model` and `signal` — it drops `maxRetries`. Embedded prompt lock window injects `maxRetries: 0` but the native OpenAI transport never forwards it to the SDK, so SDK retries (default `maxRetries=2`) still occur during the released lock window, widening the race. Additionally, `queueMessage` steering writes bypass `withOwnedSessionTranscriptWrites`, causing fingerprint mismatch → `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError` → generic failure branch → silent message loss.
- **Exact steps and commands run after this patch**:

```
$ docker run --rm -e CI=true -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app node:24-bookworm bash /proof.sh
=== PR #89039 Real Behavior Proof (v5) ===
Environment: Docker container, v24.16.0, aarch64, "Debian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
Commit: 80bbe803e2

--- Installing dependencies (pnpm) ---
install exit: 0
Done in 47s using pnpm v11.2.2

--- Test 1: Native OpenAI transport forwards maxRetries ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  241 passed (241)

--- Test 2: Embedded prompt lock defaults + override ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  48 passed (48)

--- Test 3: Takeover error handling + unwrapping (agent-runner-execution) ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  184 passed (184)

--- Test 4: failover-error classification ---
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  83 passed (83)

--- Test 5: SDK transport paths (openai-completions + openai-responses-shared) ---
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
 Tests  32 passed (32)

--- Test 6: Code path verification ---
buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions accepts+forwards maxRetries:
1843:  maxRetries?: number,
1844:): { signal?: AbortSignal; timeout?: number; maxRetries?: number } | undefined {
1846:  if (timeout === undefined && !signal && maxRetries === undefined) {
1852:    ...(maxRetries !== undefined ? { maxRetries } : {}),
1940:          options?.maxRetries,
2380:          options?.maxRetries,
2614:          buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions(model, options?.signal, options?.maxRetries),

Embedded retry default (maxRetries:0 BEFORE options spread):
1083:    return innerStreamFn(model, context, { maxRetries: 0, ...requestOptions });
```

- **Evidence after fix**: All 588 tests pass across the 6 affected test suites in a clean Docker environment.
  - **Transport retry suppression proven end-to-end**: A loopback regression test in `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` starts a mock server returning retryable HTTP 500 errors, passes `maxRetries: 0` through the full native completions transport path, and asserts exactly 1 request is received — confirming the SDK does not retry when `maxRetries: 0` is forwarded through the native transport.
  - Default SDK retry behavior preserved for non-embedded callers (no `maxRetries` in default request options).
  - Embedded prompt lock window correctly injects `maxRetries: 0` as a default that configured `settings.retry.provider.maxRetries` can override.
  - Native OpenAI transport (`buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions`) correctly forwards `maxRetries` to all 3 SDK call sites.
  - Pure takeover errors produce visible resend guidance. Wrapped prompt errors are unwrapped and classified normally.
- **What was not tested**: No live multi-user deployment against a real upstream provider with an actual network-level ECONNRESET was run. The takeover path proof uses a real on-disk session file, a real local HTTP model server, the real transport, and the real fence (the error is organic), with only the upstream provider socket and the "second user" steering write synthesized; the supplementary transport proof relies on unit/loopback tests in a real Docker container.

## Tests

All affected test suites pass (Docker `node:24-bookworm`, aarch64):

| Test File | Tests |
|-----------|-------|
| `agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts` (real-setup takeover proof) | 1 |
| `openai-transport-stream.test.ts` | 241 |
| `attempt.session-lock.test.ts` | 48 |
| `agent-runner-execution.test.ts` | 184 |
| `failover-error.test.ts` | 83 |
| `openai-completions.test.ts` + `openai-responses-shared.test.ts` | 32 |

New test coverage includes:
- Real-setup organic session-takeover proof: real session file + real HTTP model server + real transport + real fence producing `EmbeddedAttemptSessionTakeoverError`, asserting the after-fix resend guidance (and its absence at the parent)
- `buildOpenAISdkRequestOptions` forwards `maxRetries` when provided, omits when undefined
- `maxRetries`-only call returns an object (not `undefined`)
- Loopback end-to-end test: mock 500 server + `maxRetries: 0` → exactly 1 request (no SDK retry)
- `installEmbeddedPromptRetryDefault` default behavior (no caller options → maxRetries:0)
- Explicit `maxRetries` override preserved over embedded default
- Takeover detection during owned steering writes
- User-facing resend message for pure session takeover
- Preserved prompt error unwrapping from cleanup takeover
- Backward compatibility for `assertSessionFileFence` without phase parameter

## Changed Files

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`
- `src/agents/failover-error.ts`
- `src/agents/failover-error.test.ts`
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts`
- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-takeover.real.test.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.ts`
- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`

Fixes #87180

target.title: block unauthorized Telegram DM text from prompt context
target.body:
## Summary

- Move Telegram DM access enforcement before dispatch dedupe, reply-chain recording, prompt-context selection, and message processing for normal inbound DMs.
- Apply the same passive allow check to edited private Telegram messages before they can update the conversation cache.
- Preserve `direct.<chatId>.requireTopic` behavior by dropping root DMs before pairing challenges, and add focused regressions for blocked text, edited text, and topic-required root DMs.

## Issue Scope

Fixes #91209.

The local reproducer for this branch is slightly narrower than the originally reported delivery-failure chain:

- Not proven here: unauthorized text DM -> agent turn -> reply to unauthorized user.
- Proven here: unauthorized text DM -> recorded in Telegram conversation cache -> authorized user sends a later DM -> that authorized agent turn can include the unauthorized text in prompt context.

So this fixes a pre-dispatch state and prompt-context leak. The early gate is still necessary because blocked Telegram DM text must not be recorded before authorization.

## Verification

target.title: Security: add configurable unpaired DM responses
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: unknown DM senders in pairing mode always received a branded OpenClaw reply with a pairing code.
- Why it matters: operators could not choose a silent or de-branded pairing posture without changing DM policy semantics.
- What changed: added `unpairedResponse` config support (`silent | code-only | branded`) and wired it through the shared pairing helpers and core DM pairing flows.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): default behavior remains `branded`, and non-pairing DM policies are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #29945
- Related #29945

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New optional `unpairedResponse` config for DM pairing flows on core messaging channels.
- `silent` suppresses the automatic reply, `code-only` removes OpenClaw branding, and `branded` preserves current behavior.
- Defaults stay backward compatible: existing installs continue to send the branded pairing reply unless configured otherwise.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp, Discord, Telegram, Slack, Signal, iMessage, LINE, plugin runtime pairing callers
- Relevant config (redacted): default config plus `unpairedResponse` overrides in channel config

### Steps

1. Configure a DM channel with `dmPolicy: pairing`.
2. Send a DM from an unapproved sender.
3. Set `unpairedResponse` to `silent`, `code-only`, and `branded` and compare the emitted reply behavior.

### Expected

- Pairing mode should respect the configured reply style without changing allowlist/pairing authorization behavior.

### Actual

- Before this change, pairing replies were always branded and could not be suppressed or de-branded.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: new `buildPairingReply` modes and silent-path behavior in `issuePairingChallenge`; end-to-end build completed after wiring core channel call sites.
- Edge cases checked: silent mode skips outbound reply send; code-only mode still includes the pairing code and approval command; branded mode remains unchanged.
- What you did **not** verify: live channel delivery against external services.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: remove `unpairedResponse` from config to fall back to the existing branded reply, or revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: channel config entries using `unpairedResponse`; pairing helper changes in `src/pairing/*`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: pairing requests created without a reply when `unpairedResponse` is not set, or null reply handling regressions in DM pairing paths.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a DM pairing caller could miss the new nullable reply contract and fail to send or crash.
  - Mitigation: updated the shared helper plus direct call sites, covered the shared helper with tests, and validated with `pnpm build` and `pnpm check`.

target.title: Security: harden gateway timeouts and auth store sealing
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `GatewayClient.request()` could hang indefinitely, auth stores were always plaintext at rest, and mirrored transcript writes did not re-assert restrictive permissions after append.
- Why it matters: hung gateway RPCs leak pending state and stall callers; optional at-rest sealing gives operators a low-friction hardening path for OpenClaw-owned auth JSON stores.
- What changed: added bounded gateway request timeouts, added optional `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` sealing for `auth-profiles.json` and legacy `oauth.json`, added regression coverage, and re-applied `0600` perms after mirrored transcript writes.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not implement full session transcript encryption because `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` and multiple local readers still depend on raw JSONL session files.
- Thanks @alamine42 for the original hardening report, and @vincentkoc for review context.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #4954
- Related #7604

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `GatewayClient` requests now fail with `gateway request timeout for <method>` instead of hanging forever.
- When `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` is set, OpenClaw-owned auth stores are written in sealed form and require that same env var to be read.
- Mirrored transcript writes now best-effort reset the transcript file mode to `0600` after append.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  Optional auth-store sealing adds a new env-based operator hardening path. The format is backward compatible because plaintext reads still work when sealing is not enabled, and encrypted reads fail closed with a warning if the passphrase env var is missing.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: n/a
- Integration/channel (if any): n/a
- Relevant config (redacted): optional `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE`

### Steps

1. Run the focused test suite for gateway client, auth store, oauth migration, and transcript paths.
2. Run repo-level `pnpm check`.
3. Run repo-level `pnpm build`.

### Expected

- Gateway requests time out cleanly.
- Auth stores round-trip in sealed form when `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` is set.
- Build and lint stay green.

### Actual

- Verified locally.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: focused Vitest coverage for gateway timeouts, sealed auth-store save/load, sealed oauth migration, and transcript append behavior.
- Edge cases checked: timeout cleanup removes pending entries; encrypted auth stores do not contain plaintext secrets; legacy oauth migration still works when the source file is sealed.
- What you did **not** verify: full transcript encryption, because it is explicitly out of scope for this PR.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
  Operators who want auth-store sealing can set `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` before writing credentials and keep it set for future reads.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: unset `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` before writing new auth store files, or revert the branch.
- Files/config to restore: `auth-profiles.json` / `oauth.json` from backup if an operator loses the passphrase.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: warning that an encrypted auth store requires `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE` when the env var is missing.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operator enables auth-store sealing and later starts OpenClaw without `OPENCLAW_PASSPHRASE`.
  - Mitigation: reads fail closed with a targeted warning instead of silently parsing garbage.
- Risk: timeout defaults could surface latent long-running RPC assumptions.
  - Mitigation: timeout is bounded but configurable per client/request.

target.title: feat(feeds): add read-only feed discovery
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a bundled `feeds` extension for curated skill/plugin catalog sources and read-only discovery.

This PR establishes the primitive without install enforcement:

- Adds `plugins.entries.feeds.config.sources[]` for configured feed documents.
- Adds feed source Doctor/health validation for ids, URLs, pinning, duplicate ids, and malformed config.
- Adds `openclaw feeds sources`, `feeds list`, and `feeds search`.
- Supports local `file://` and remote `https://` feed URLs with optional pinned `sha256:<hex>` integrity.
- Surfaces install hints in read-only discovery output, but does not install or block anything.
- Adds plugin docs and plugin inventory wiring.

## Example syntax

```jsonc
{
  "plugins": {
    "entries": {
      "feeds": {
        "enabled": true,
        "config": {
          "sources": [
            {
              "id": "company-approved",
              "url": "https://feeds.example.com/openclaw/feed.json",
              "trust": "pinned",
              "integrity": "sha256:..."
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Commands

```bash
openclaw feeds sources
openclaw feeds list
openclaw feeds search calendar --type plugin
openclaw doctor --lint
```

## Notes

This is intentionally read-only. It does not add ClawHub-native feeds, background sync, automatic installation, or runtime enforcement.

## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts src/flows/bundled-health-checks.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs --noEmit
pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/feeds/src/cli.ts extensions/feeds/src/cli.test.ts extensions/feeds/src/feed-document.ts extensions/feeds/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/feeds.md docs/plugins/reference.md
git diff --check
node scripts/generate-plugin-inventory-doc.mjs --check
```

Focused stack proof on the final condensed branch: 243 tests passed.

## Feed PR stack

1. openclaw/openclaw#87824 - read-only feed discovery
2. openclaw/openclaw#87825 - approved feed installs
3. openclaw/openclaw#87826 - feed catalog policy conformance
4. openclaw/openclaw#87827 - feed lifecycle tooling
5. openclaw/openclaw#88732 - native feed search defaults and policy checks
6. openclaw/clawhub#2460 - ClawHub root feed lanes

The stack keeps OpenClaw as a feed consumer. ClawHub root feeds are producer infrastructure; enterprise or tenant feeds can be produced elsewhere using the same schema.

RFC draft: https://github.com/giodl73-repo/rfcs/blob/feeds-rfc-draft/rfcs/0004-feeds.md
